steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Start with $$\left(\frac{2}{3}x+4\right)-\left(\frac{5}{6}x-2\right).$$ Change the signs in the second parentheses:
$$\frac{2}{3}x+4-\frac{5}{6}x+2.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The constants give $4+2=6$. The coefficient of $x$ is $$\frac{2}{3}-\frac{5}{6}=\frac{4}{6}-\frac{5}{6}=-\frac{1}{6}.$$ So the expression is $$-\frac{1}{6}x+6.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That is choice A. The common-denominator step shows why the $x$ coefficient is negative, so A is correct.
